free_text: Measurement of the angular distribution of fission
fragments using a PPAC assembly at CERN n TOF.
Fission 2013 - Fifth International Workshop on Nuclear
University of York, Heslington, York, UK.
Very intense and pulsed neutron beam.
Six samples of 232Th, one sample of 237Np, reference
samples of 235U and 238U of 8cm diameter were built at
the IPN-Orsay (France), produced by molecular plating
of the oxides ThO(2) and UO(3) on Al foils of
2.5 microm thick for U and Np, 0.7 microm for Th .
The chemical composition of the sample and backing
for 235U have been well characterized by
Isotopic impurities of 238U (6.28% in number of atoms),
sample, being negligible the contaminants in the other
185 m flight path between the spallation target
Fission events were recognized by back to back
detection in coincidence in two position-sensitive
Chamber containing a stack of 10 Parallel Plate
Avalanche Counters interleaved with 9 targets. Fission
fragments emitted by each target are detected by the
2 PPACs. PPAC is a gaseous detector operating in
proportional mode at low pressure. The chamber is
entirely filled with C(3)F(8) at 4 mbar with a steady
The detection efficiency, depending mostly on the
stopping of fission fragments in backings and
electrodes, has been computed with a Geant4 simulation
and validated by the comparison to the measured case
of 235U below 3 keV where the emission is isotropic.
Parallel Plate Avalanche Counters oriented 45 deg with
respect to the neutron beam direction.
